prompt:
Ticket: order fulfillment core for the warehouse service. TypeScript, bare node, zero deps, node --test. Two public modules (split anything else out as you see fit):

inventory.ts — export class Inventory. Plain stock ledger, skus are arbitrary strings that spring into existence at first touch (unknown sku reads as 0). receive(sku, qty); onHand(sku); available(sku) = on hand minus reserved; reserve(sku, qty) strict — over available throws naming the sku; reserveUpTo(sku, qty) -> number actually reserved (may be 0); release(sku, qty) puts a hold back; commit(sku, qty) ships held units (on hand AND reservation drop). Over-release / over-commit throw naming the sku. Every qty must be a positive integer or the call throws mentioning the quantity.

orders.ts — export class OrderEngine, constructed with an Inventory. ALL stock arrivals go through engine.receive(sku, qty) so backorders get filled: after booking the stock it walks that sku's backorders oldest-placed-order-first, reserving what it can.

place({ id, lines: [{ sku, qty }] }) — reserve what's available per line (reserveUpTo), shortfall becomes a backorder. Duplicate order id -> throw naming it; empty lines -> throw mentioning lines; the same sku twice in one order -> throw naming the sku; bad qty -> quantity error. order(id) -> DEEP COPY snapshot { id, status, lines: [{ sku, ordered, reserved, shipped, backordered }] } in the order's line order. status: 'open' (nothing shipped yet), 'partial', 'fulfilled' (every ordered unit shipped), 'cancelled'. ship(id) -> partial shipments are the whole point: commits and ships every currently-reserved unit, returns { order, lines } listing only skus that actually shipped; nothing reserved -> throw saying there's nothing to ship. cancel(id) releases all reservations, deletes the order's backorders (later stock must not resurrect them), keeps already-shipped units shipped, marks it cancelled — and the freed stock goes straight to other orders' waiting backorders, oldest order first. Cancelling a fulfilled or already-cancelled order, or shipping a cancelled one, throws naming the order. Unknown ids anywhere throw naming the id.

audit() -> copies of the audit trail, one global seq from 1, only SUCCESSFUL operations recorded, shapes exactly: { seq, type: 'stock-received', sku, qty } | { seq, type: 'order-placed', order } | { seq, type: 'reserved', order, sku, qty } | { seq, type: 'backordered', order, sku, qty } | { seq, type: 'shipped', order, lines: [{ sku, qty }] } | { seq, type: 'cancelled', order, released: [{ sku, qty }] }. place logs order-placed then, per line in order, reserved (if any) and backordered (if any); engine.receive logs stock-received then the reserved entries it triggered; cancel logs cancelled (released lists only skus with freed holds) then any reallocation reserved entries.

Contract = the acceptance tests: node --test test_inventory.ts test_orders.ts test_audit.ts. Hands off the test files.
